items.name: WTF
items.description: Classic Mexican sweet bread with a crumbly sugar shell in vanilla, chocolate, or strawberry.

items.name: Semitas
items.description: Dense, sweet bread made with piloncillo and anise seeds. A traditional Salvadoran staple.

items.name: Pan de Yema
items.description: Egg-enriched sweet rolls with a golden crust, pillowy interior, and hint of orange zest.

items.name: Cuernos
items.description: Horn-shaped pastry with a flaky exterior and sweet cream or chocolate filling.

items.name: Pastelitos de Carne
items.description: Flaky hand pies filled with seasoned ground beef, potatoes, and spices. Baked golden.

items.name: Pastelitos de Queso
items.description: Hand pies filled with cream cheese and jalapeño. Crispy exterior, melty center.

items.name: Empanadas de Leche
items.description: Fried dough filled with sweet vanilla custard and rolled in cinnamon sugar.

items.name: Empanadas de Loroco
items.description: Savory Salvadoran empanadas filled with loroco flower and cheese. Order them if you see them.

items.name: Quesadilla Salvadoreña
items.description: Rice flour cake with parmesan, sesame seeds, and sour cream. Sweet, savory, and spongy.

items.name: Tres Leches Cake
items.description: Classic tres leches soaked in three milks with fresh whipped cream and cinnamon topping.

items.name: Quinceañera Cakes
items.description: Custom tiered cakes for quinceañeras. Call to talk through the details.

items.name: Birthday Cakes
items.description: Fully custom cakes in your choice of flavor, filling, and decoration. Order 72hrs in advance.

items.name: Café de Olla
items.description: Traditional Mexican coffee brewed with cinnamon and piloncillo in a clay pot.

items.name: Horchata de Morro
items.description: Salvadoran horchata made with morro seeds, cinnamon, and sesame. Not the rice kind.

items.name: Atol de Elote
items.description: Sweet corn drink made the traditional way. Warm, thick, and good.

items.name: Fresh Licuados
items.description: Blended fresh fruit drinks with milk or water. Ask about today's seasonal fruits.
